title: Nashua, N.H. man arrested after allegedly assaulting 3-month-old son
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Drew Babcock, 36, was arrested in Nashua, NH, on charges of assaulting his 3-month-old son, causing serious injuries.
The child sustained multiple fractures including ribs, femurs, and clavicle; Babcock is charged with eight counts of first degree domestic assault.
Following his not guilty plea, Babcock remains in custody pending a bail hearing at Hillsborough County Superior Court South.

title: Here’s how much a remote per diem worker makes north of Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A 66-year-old per diem worker in Hamilton-Wenham earns $27,000 yearly, working remotely as a senior project manager.
Francine transitioned from full-time to remote per diem work in clinical research following a career as a social worker.
Her biggest expense is healthcare, and she supplements her income with a pension and a 403(b) to maintain her lifestyle.

title: NTSB: Flight instructor in fatal Mass. plane crash had ‘do not fly’ meds in system
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The NTSB reported that flight instructor William Hampton had 'do not fly' medications in his system during a 2024 Massachusetts crash.
The 2024 crash near Leyden killed three people, including the instructor William Hampton and Fly Lugu Flight School owner Fredrika Ballard.
Hampton's medical history revealed drug dependencies; his impairments likely contributed to losing control of the aircraft, the NTSB stated.

title: WATCH: Bagpiper performs on St. Patrick’s Day in Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Bagpiper Nate Silva performed live at Harry's Bar and Grill on St. Patrick's Day in Boston.
Silva played at over 30 spots in four days, including multiple locations under Harry's network of bars.
Bagpiper Nate Silva, also known as @passionpiping, has played Scottish bagpipes since age 11 and performs nationwide.

title: 50 ways to make the most of spring in Massachusetts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth closed after over 20 years, with a new restaurant opening in the space in April.
Boston is set to host the FIFA World Cup, prompting hikes in hotel rates and Airbnb recruiting new hosts with a $750 bonus.
JetBlue discontinued its seasonal Boston-Asheville route due to low performance, refunding affected customers.

title: Tom Brady brought jokes, nostalgia, and a comeback tease to Jimmy Fallon ahead of flag football debut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tom Brady appeared on 'The Tonight Show' to discuss his flag football debut and life post-retirement.
Brady teased a comeback, saying he will play in the Fanatics Flag Football Classic in Los Angeles.
Brady and Fallon shared jokes about their high school era and practiced quarterback throws on the show.

title: This is the current state of the TSA at Logan Airport
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: About 30 TSA employees have quit at Logan Airport due to the prolonged government shutdown.
Despite staffing challenges, Logan Airport has so far avoided long security lines affecting other major airports.
Massport is testing new technology to provide real-time TSA wait times, independent of current staffing issues.

title: Man found dead in Brockton was in fight, then run over, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lianes Brandao, 28, was found dead after a fight and hit-and-run incident in Brockton, say authorities.
Police discovered Brandao in a parking lot near Cardoso Cafe; he was pronounced dead after being airlifted to Boston.
The suspect's vehicle, a gray 2013 Jeep Compass, was identified and driver interviewed; investigation by state and local police continues.

title: Pregnant, battling cancer — now she’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jennifer Capone Haberek, from Newton, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Ellie Fund.
Diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer during pregnancy, Haberek received significant assistance from the Ellie Fund in 2024.
Haberek aims to celebrate her two-year cancer-free benchmark by completing the 26.2-mile race in April 2026.

title: A Korean trio of restaurants heads to Allston, starting with Sanbada
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sanbada, part of a Korean restaurant trio, has opened in Allston with two more concepts coming soon.
Bombbada and Jinjee, the other two restaurants in the trio, are set to open in July and mid-April, respectively.
Sanbada offers fish sets with banchan, using an Italian brick oven, and focuses on a Korean mountain-ocean menu theme.
